info.title: smplkit Jobs API
info.description: Scheduled HTTP job execution API for smplkit.

post.description:
Create a job for this account.

The caller supplies the job's id as `data.id`. Ids are unique within an
account and immutable. The job's kind follows from its `schedule`: omit the
schedule for a permanent **manual** job (triggered on demand), give a cron
expression for a **recurring** job, or a datetime / `now` for a **one-off**
job. A recurring or manual job supplies `environments` to choose where it
runs; a recurring job begins scheduling immediately in each enabled
environment. A one-off job is created in the environment named by the
`X-Smplkit-Environment` header (implied when the credential is scoped to a
single environment); a `now` one-off enqueues its single run immediately.

parameters.name: X-Smplkit-Environment

schema.description: The environment to operate in. Names the single environment a one-off job is born in (or a manual run executes in). Optional when the credential is scoped to a single environment (which is then implied); required when the credential can reach several environments and the choice is otherwise ambiguous. Ignored for a recurring job, whose environments come from its `environments` map.
schema.title: X-Smplkit-Environment

parameters.description: The environment to operate in. Names the single environment a one-off job is born in (or a manual run executes in). Optional when the credential is scoped to a single environment (which is then implied); required when the credential can reach several environments and the choice is otherwise ambiguous. Ignored for a recurring job, whose environments come from its `environments` map.

get.description:
List this account's jobs.

Default sort is `name` ascending. Sort by `name`, `created_at`, or
`updated_at`, ascending or descending (prefix `-` for descending). By
default the list omits transient one-off jobs (request `filter[kind]=one_off`
to see them). Filter with `filter[kind]` (`recurring` / `manual` /
`one_off`), `filter[scheduled]` (jobs with an upcoming fire in some
environment — the feed for an upcoming-runs view, which includes one-offs),
and `filter[name]` (case-insensitive substring); filters compose with AND.
Each job reports its per-environment enablement and `next_run_at` inside its
`environments` map; a scoped caller sees that map narrowed to the
environments it may access.

parameters.name: filter[kind]

schema.description: Restrict to a single job kind: `recurring`, `manual`, or `one_off`. By default one-off jobs are omitted (they are transient and short-lived); request `filter[kind]=one_off` to list them.
schema.title: Filter[Kind]

parameters.description: Restrict to a single job kind: `recurring`, `manual`, or `one_off`. By default one-off jobs are omitted (they are transient and short-lived); request `filter[kind]=one_off` to list them.

parameters.name: filter[scheduled]

schema.description: When `true`, list only jobs that have an upcoming fire in at least one environment (a recurring job's next occurrence, or a pending future one-off) — the feed for an upcoming-runs view; this includes one-off jobs. When `false`, list only jobs with no upcoming fire.
schema.title: Filter[Scheduled]

parameters.description: When `true`, list only jobs that have an upcoming fire in at least one environment (a recurring job's next occurrence, or a pending future one-off) — the feed for an upcoming-runs view; this includes one-off jobs. When `false`, list only jobs with no upcoming fire.

parameters.name: filter[name]

schema.description: Case-insensitive substring match on the job `name` (matches when the name contains the given text).
schema.title: Filter[Name]

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on the job `name` (matches when the name contains the given text).

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Field to sort by. Prefix with `-` for descending order. Default: `name`. Allowed values: `created_at`, `-created_at`, `name`, `-name`, `updated_at`, `-updated_at`.
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Field to sort by. Prefix with `-` for descending order. Default: `name`. Allowed values: `created_at`, `-created_at`, `name`, `-name`, `updated_at`, `-updated_at`.

parameters.name: page[number]

schema.description: 1-based page number to return. Optional; defaults to `1` when omitted. Must be `>= 1` — requests with a smaller value are rejected with a 400 error.
schema.title: Page[Number]

parameters.description: 1-based page number to return. Optional; defaults to `1` when omitted. Must be `>= 1` — requests with a smaller value are rejected with a 400 error.

parameters.name: page[size]

schema.description: Number of items per page. Optional; defaults to `1000` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.
schema.title: Page[Size]

parameters.description: Number of items per page. Optional; defaults to `1000` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.

parameters.name: meta[total]

schema.description: When `true`, the response's `meta.pagination` block includes `total` (the total number of matching items across all pages) and `total_pages`. Computing these requires an extra `COUNT` query, so omit (or pass `false`) when the totals are not needed. Defaults to `false`.
schema.title: Meta[Total]

parameters.description: When `true`, the response's `meta.pagination` block includes `total` (the total number of matching items across all pages) and `total_pages`. Computing these requires an extra `COUNT` query, so omit (or pass `false`) when the totals are not needed. Defaults to `false`.

get.description: Retrieve a single job by its id.

put.description:
Replace an existing job. Every writable field is overwritten.

The job's kind is re-derived from the new `schedule` (omit it for a manual
job). Set enablement per environment via the `environments` map (a recurring
or manual job), or by recreating a one-off job in the desired environment.
Each environment may carry its own cron `schedule` override (recurring jobs
only). Editing a recurring environment's effective schedule recomputes its
next fire time; an edit that leaves it unchanged preserves the existing
cadence.

parameters.name: X-Smplkit-Environment

schema.description: The environment to operate in. Names the single environment a one-off job is born in (or a manual run executes in). Optional when the credential is scoped to a single environment (which is then implied); required when the credential can reach several environments and the choice is otherwise ambiguous. Ignored for a recurring job, whose environments come from its `environments` map.
schema.title: X-Smplkit-Environment

parameters.description: The environment to operate in. Names the single environment a one-off job is born in (or a manual run executes in). Optional when the credential is scoped to a single environment (which is then implied); required when the credential can reach several environments and the choice is otherwise ambiguous. Ignored for a recurring job, whose environments come from its `environments` map.

delete.description: Delete a job. Its run history is retained; the id may be reused later.

post.description:
Trigger one immediate run of the job in a specified environment (a
`MANUAL` run).

This is the primary execution path for a manual job and is also usable ad
hoc for a recurring job ("run now"). The job's schedule and enabled state are
untouched. The run executes in the environment named by the
`X-Smplkit-Environment` header; when the job is enabled in exactly one
environment that environment is used, and a single-environment credential
implies it. The environment must be one the job is **enabled** in (409
otherwise). The run executes the job's effective configuration for that
environment. It is enqueued and executed by the worker; if the account is
over its run allotment the run will fail with reason `QUOTA_EXCEEDED` rather
than being rejected here.

parameters.name: X-Smplkit-Environment

schema.description: The environment to operate in. Names the single environment a one-off job is born in (or a manual run executes in). Optional when the credential is scoped to a single environment (which is then implied); required when the credential can reach several environments and the choice is otherwise ambiguous. Ignored for a recurring job, whose environments come from its `environments` map.
schema.title: X-Smplkit-Environment

parameters.description: The environment to operate in. Names the single environment a one-off job is born in (or a manual run executes in). Optional when the credential is scoped to a single environment (which is then implied); required when the credential can reach several environments and the choice is otherwise ambiguous. Ignored for a recurring job, whose environments come from its `environments` map.

get.description:
List runs for this account (cursor paginated).

Default sort is `-created_at` (newest first). Sort by `created_at`,
`started_at`, `finished_at`, `scheduled_for`, `status`, `job`, or
`total_duration_ms`, ascending or descending (prefix `-` for descending).
Keep the same `sort` value across paginated requests so the cursor stays
consistent. Runs that have not reached the relevant lifecycle point
(`started_at`, `finished_at`, `scheduled_for`, `total_duration_ms` unset)
sort to the end regardless of direction.

Filters compose with AND:

- `filter[job]={id}` — a single job's run history.
- `filter[status]` — one state or a comma-separated list (any-of).
- `filter[environment]` — one environment key or a comma-separated list
  (any-of); omitted covers every environment you can access.
- `filter[created_at]` / `filter[started_at]` / `filter[finished_at]` /
  `filter[scheduled_for]` — half-open `[start,end)` date ranges (see each
  parameter for the interval syntax).

parameters.name: filter[status]

schema.description: Restrict to runs in the given lifecycle state. One of `PENDING`, `RUNNING`, `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, or a comma-separated list of them to match any (e.g. `SUCCEEDED,FAILED`).
schema.title: Filter[Status]

parameters.description: Restrict to runs in the given lifecycle state. One of `PENDING`, `RUNNING`, `SUCCEEDED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, or a comma-separated list of them to match any (e.g. `SUCCEEDED,FAILED`).

parameters.name: filter[environment]

schema.description: Comma-separated list of environment keys to scope results to (e.g. `production,staging`). When omitted, results cover every environment you can access.
schema.title: Filter[Environment]

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of environment keys to scope results to (e.g. `production,staging`). When omitted, results cover every environment you can access.

parameters.name: filter[created_at]

schema.description: Restrict to runs whose `created_at` falls in a half-open `[start,end)` interval. Bounds are ISO-8601 timestamps; `*` leaves a bound open. The leading bracket is `[` (inclusive) or `(` (exclusive) and the trailing bracket is `]` (inclusive) or `)` (exclusive). Example: `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,2026-06-08T00:00:00Z)` selects the first week of June; `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,*)` is everything from then onward.
schema.title: Filter[Created At]

parameters.description: Restrict to runs whose `created_at` falls in a half-open `[start,end)` interval. Bounds are ISO-8601 timestamps; `*` leaves a bound open. The leading bracket is `[` (inclusive) or `(` (exclusive) and the trailing bracket is `]` (inclusive) or `)` (exclusive). Example: `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,2026-06-08T00:00:00Z)` selects the first week of June; `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,*)` is everything from then onward.

parameters.name: filter[started_at]

schema.description: Restrict to runs whose `started_at` falls in a half-open `[start,end)` interval. Bounds are ISO-8601 timestamps; `*` leaves a bound open. The leading bracket is `[` (inclusive) or `(` (exclusive) and the trailing bracket is `]` (inclusive) or `)` (exclusive). Example: `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,2026-06-08T00:00:00Z)` selects the first week of June; `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,*)` is everything from then onward.
schema.title: Filter[Started At]

parameters.description: Restrict to runs whose `started_at` falls in a half-open `[start,end)` interval. Bounds are ISO-8601 timestamps; `*` leaves a bound open. The leading bracket is `[` (inclusive) or `(` (exclusive) and the trailing bracket is `]` (inclusive) or `)` (exclusive). Example: `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,2026-06-08T00:00:00Z)` selects the first week of June; `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,*)` is everything from then onward.

parameters.name: filter[finished_at]

schema.description: Restrict to runs whose `finished_at` falls in a half-open `[start,end)` interval. Bounds are ISO-8601 timestamps; `*` leaves a bound open. The leading bracket is `[` (inclusive) or `(` (exclusive) and the trailing bracket is `]` (inclusive) or `)` (exclusive). Example: `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,2026-06-08T00:00:00Z)` selects the first week of June; `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,*)` is everything from then onward.
schema.title: Filter[Finished At]

parameters.description: Restrict to runs whose `finished_at` falls in a half-open `[start,end)` interval. Bounds are ISO-8601 timestamps; `*` leaves a bound open. The leading bracket is `[` (inclusive) or `(` (exclusive) and the trailing bracket is `]` (inclusive) or `)` (exclusive). Example: `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,2026-06-08T00:00:00Z)` selects the first week of June; `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,*)` is everything from then onward.

parameters.name: filter[scheduled_for]

schema.description: Restrict to runs whose `scheduled_for` falls in a half-open `[start,end)` interval. Bounds are ISO-8601 timestamps; `*` leaves a bound open. The leading bracket is `[` (inclusive) or `(` (exclusive) and the trailing bracket is `]` (inclusive) or `)` (exclusive). Example: `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,2026-06-08T00:00:00Z)` selects the first week of June; `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,*)` is everything from then onward.
schema.title: Filter[Scheduled For]

parameters.description: Restrict to runs whose `scheduled_for` falls in a half-open `[start,end)` interval. Bounds are ISO-8601 timestamps; `*` leaves a bound open. The leading bracket is `[` (inclusive) or `(` (exclusive) and the trailing bracket is `]` (inclusive) or `)` (exclusive). Example: `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,2026-06-08T00:00:00Z)` selects the first week of June; `[2026-06-01T00:00:00Z,*)` is everything from then onward.

parameters.name: page[size]

schema.description: Number of runs per page. Optional; defaults to `50` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.
schema.title: Page[Size]

parameters.description: Number of runs per page. Optional; defaults to `50` when omitted. Must be between `1` and `1000` inclusive — requests outside that range are rejected with a 400 error.

parameters.name: sort

schema.description: Field to sort by. Prefix with `-` for descending order. Default: `-created_at`. Allowed values: `created_at`, `-created_at`, `finished_at`, `-finished_at`, `job`, `-job`, `scheduled_for`, `-scheduled_for`, `started_at`, `-started_at`, `status`, `-status`, `total_duration_ms`, `-total_duration_ms`.
schema.title: Sort

parameters.description: Field to sort by. Prefix with `-` for descending order. Default: `-created_at`. Allowed values: `created_at`, `-created_at`, `finished_at`, `-finished_at`, `job`, `-job`, `scheduled_for`, `-scheduled_for`, `started_at`, `-started_at`, `status`, `-status`, `total_duration_ms`, `-total_duration_ms`.

get.description: Retrieve a single run by its id.

post.description:
Cancel a pending or running run.

Returns `409` if the run is already in a terminal state. Canceling a
running run stops us tracking it, but the HTTP request may already be in
flight — cancel means "stop tracking," not "guaranteed it didn't happen."

Spawn a new run from a prior run, using the job's current configuration.

Returns `409` if the run's parent job has been deleted.

get.description:
Report this account's current-period usage against its plan allotments.

`runs_used` is the number of runs metered so far this calendar month;
`active_jobs` is the number of permanent jobs (recurring + manual), which is
what the plan's job limit bounds (one-off jobs do not count).

value.title: Value
value.description: Header value. Stored encrypted at rest; returned as plaintext on `GET`.

HttpHeader.title: HttpHeader
HttpHeader.description:
A single HTTP header attached to an outbound request.

Header values are encrypted at the application layer before
persistence regardless of header name; the wire representation here
is always plaintext on both the request and the response, so a
`GET → mutate → PUT` round-trip preserves header values without
requiring the customer to re-enter secrets.

type.title: Type
type.description: Job type. Only `http` is supported today.

schedule.title: Schedule
schedule.description: The base schedule every environment inherits unless it overrides it, and the field that determines the job's `kind`. Omit it (or send `null`) to create a permanent **manual** job that never auto-fires and runs only when triggered. Provide a 5-field cron expression evaluated in the job's `timezone` (UTC by default) for a **recurring** job, an ISO-8601 datetime for a **one-off** run at that instant, or the literal `now` for a one-off run as soon as possible. A datetime or `now` job disables itself after it fires.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: IANA timezone the cron `schedule` is evaluated in (e.g. `America/New_York`); null or omitted means UTC. The base every environment inherits unless it sets its own `timezone`. The cron fires on this zone's wall clock (DST-aware) while `next_run_at` is still reported as a UTC instant. Only valid on a recurring (cron) job — it cannot be set on a manual or one-off job.

configuration.description: The HTTP request to perform, including method, url, headers, body, and timeout.

environments.title: Environments
environments.description: Per-environment overrides keyed by environment key (e.g. `production`, `staging`). Each entry sets `enabled` (whether the job is enabled — scheduled, for a recurring job, or triggerable, for a manual job — in that environment), an optional `schedule` override (a cron expression for recurring jobs; omit to inherit the base `schedule`), an optional `timezone` override (an IANA zone for recurring jobs; omit to inherit the base `timezone`, else UTC), and an optional `configuration` override (omit to inherit the base `configuration`); it also reports the read-only `next_run_at` for that environment. A job with no entry for an environment is disabled there. For a recurring or manual job, supply this map to choose where it runs. For a one-off job, the environment it is created in is recorded here automatically — name it with the `X-Smplkit-Environment` header. Every referenced environment must exist for the account.

concurrency_policy.title: Concurrency Policy
concurrency_policy.description: How overlapping runs are handled. `ALLOW` (the only value today) permits them.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: How the job runs, derived from its base `schedule`: `recurring` for a cron schedule (fires on a repeating cadence), `manual` for no schedule (never auto-fires; runs only when triggered), or `one_off` for a `now` or datetime schedule (runs a single time, then is spent).

updated_at.title: Updated At
updated_at.description: When the job was last modified.

deleted_at.title: Deleted At
deleted_at.description: When the job was deleted. `null` for active jobs.

version.title: Version
version.description: Monotonic counter incremented on every update, starting at 1.

Job.title: Job
Job.description:
A unit of work: an HTTP request, run on a schedule or triggered on demand.

The job is the definition; each time it fires the service records a run
capturing the request, response, timing, and outcome. A job runs per
environment: set `environments[<env>].enabled` to enable it there, and
optionally give that environment its own `schedule` or `configuration`.

A job's `kind` follows from its `schedule`: a **recurring** (cron) job may
be enabled in several environments at once and fires once per enabled
environment, each on its own next-fire schedule; a **manual** job (no
schedule) is permanent and never auto-fires — it runs only when triggered;
a **one-off** (`now` or a future datetime) job runs a single time in the
environment it was created in and is then spent.

JobCreateRequest.title: JobCreateRequest
JobCreateRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for creating a job (caller-supplied `data.id`).

JobCreateResource.title: JobCreateResource
JobCreateResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for creating a job (id required).

attributes.description: Warms the product cache every night at 02:00 New York time.
attributes.name: Nightly cache warm

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Whether the job schedules runs in this environment. A job runs in an environment only via this field; it is disabled in every environment by default.

schedule.title: Schedule
schedule.description: Per-environment schedule override. Omit to inherit the job's base `schedule`. When present, it must be a 5-field cron expression (e.g. `0 3 * * *`), evaluated in this environment's effective `timezone` (the per-environment override, else the base, else UTC), and is only allowed on a recurring (cron) job — it varies the cadence within that environment. It cannot appear on a manual or one-off job, and cannot change a job's kind.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: Per-environment timezone override for evaluating this environment's cron `schedule`. Omit to inherit the base `timezone` (else UTC). When present, it must be a valid IANA timezone key (e.g. `America/New_York`). Only valid on a recurring (cron) job; it may be set on an environment that inherits the base schedule (it need not also override `schedule`).

configuration.description: Per-environment HTTP request override. Omit to inherit the job's base `configuration`. When present, it fully replaces the base configuration for runs in this environment.

next_run_at.title: Next Run At
next_run_at.description: The next scheduled fire time in this environment. `null` when the environment is not enabled, or once a one-off run has fired.

JobEnvironment.title: JobEnvironment
JobEnvironment.description: Per-environment override for a job's enablement, schedule, and configuration.

method.title: Method
method.description: HTTP method used when delivering the request.

url.title: Url
url.description: Destination URL. Must be an absolute `http://` or `https://` URL with a hostname (e.g. `https://siem.example.com/in`).

headers.title: Headers
headers.description: HTTP headers attached to each request.

success_status.title: Success Status
success_status.description: HTTP response status that indicates success. Either a specific status code (e.g. `200`, `204`) or a status class (`1xx`, `2xx`, `3xx`, `4xx`, `5xx`).

tls_verify.title: Tls Verify
tls_verify.description: Whether to verify the destination server's TLS certificate against trusted certificate authorities. Defaults to `true` and should be left on for any production destination. Set to `false` only for development or short-lived testing against a destination that presents an untrusted certificate (e.g. a Splunk Cloud trial stack on `:8088` serving its default self-signed certificate). When `false`, deliveries proceed without certificate verification — they are vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks. For long-lived self-signed setups, pin the issuing CA via `ca_cert` instead of disabling verification entirely.

ca_cert.title: Ca Cert
ca_cert.description: Optional PEM-encoded certificate (or bundle) used to verify the destination server's TLS certificate, in addition to the system trust store. Use this to pin a private or self-signed CA (e.g. Splunk's default `SplunkCommonCA`) without disabling verification entirely via `tls_verify`. Must contain one or more `-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----` blocks. Ignored when `tls_verify` is `false`.

body.title: Body
body.description: Request body sent on each run. When omitted, an empty body is sent (suitable for a connectivity ping). Sent verbatim — pair with a matching `Content-Type` header. Limit 1 MiB.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: Per-run timeout in **seconds**. A run that does not complete within this many seconds fails with reason `TIMEOUT`. Bounded by your plan's maximum timeout.

JobHttpConfiguration.title: JobHttpConfiguration
JobHttpConfiguration.description:
HTTP request a job performs when it fires.

Extends the shared forwarder configuration with the two fields a scheduled
job needs beyond a forwarder.

JobRequest.title: JobRequest
JobRequest.description: JSON:API request envelope for updating a job.

JobResource.title: JobResource
JobResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for a job. The caller supplies `id` on create.

attributes.description: Warms the product cache every night at 02:00 New York time.
attributes.name: Nightly cache warm

JobResponse.title: JobResponse
JobResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response for a job.

ListMeta.title: ListMeta
ListMeta.description: Top-level ``meta`` block included on every JSON:API list response.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of matching items across all pages. Present only when the request included `meta[total]=true`.

total_pages.title: Total Pages
total_pages.description: Total number of pages at the requested page size. Present only when the request included `meta[total]=true`.

PaginationMeta.title: PaginationMeta
PaginationMeta.description:
Pagination block returned inside ``meta`` on every list response.

``page`` and ``size`` are always present and echo the parameters that
served the response (their defaults when the client omitted them).
``total`` and ``total_pages`` are present only when the request included
``meta[total]=true``.

job.title: Job
job.description: The id of the job this run belongs to.

job_version.title: Job Version
job_version.description: The job's version at the time the run executed.

environment.title: Environment
environment.description: The environment this run executed in. A scheduled run inherits the firing job-environment; a manual run is created in the environment you name with the `X-Smplkit-Environment` header; a rerun copies its source run's environment.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.description: Why the run exists: `SCHEDULE`, `MANUAL` (Run now), or `RERUN`.

rerun_of.title: Rerun Of
rerun_of.description: The source run's id; set only when `trigger` is `RERUN`.

scheduled_for.title: Scheduled For
scheduled_for.description: The intended fire time for a scheduled run; `null` for manual / rerun runs.

pending_duration_ms.title: Pending Duration Ms
pending_duration_ms.description: Milliseconds the run waited as `PENDING` before starting.

request.title: Request
request.description: Snapshot of the request that was sent (header values redacted). Forensics only.

result.title: Result
result.description: Outcome of the call. For `http`: `status`, `headers`, `body` (capped at 64 KiB), `body_truncated`, and the original `body_bytes`.

Run.title: Run
Run.description: One occurrence of a job executing.

next.title: Next
next.description: URL of the next page, or `null`.

page_size.title: Page Size
page_size.description: Number of runs returned per page.

RunListMeta.title: RunListMeta
RunListMeta.description: Cursor-pagination meta for the runs list (ADR-014 high-cardinality exception).

RunListResponse.title: RunListResponse
RunListResponse.description: JSON:API collection response for runs (cursor paginated).

RunResource.title: RunResource
RunResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for a run (server-assigned UUID id).

RunResponse.title: RunResponse
RunResponse.description: JSON:API single-resource response for a run.

period.title: Period
period.description: The usage period this report covers, as `YYYY-MM` (UTC).

runs_used.title: Runs Used
runs_used.description: Runs metered so far this period.

runs_included.title: Runs Included
runs_included.description: Runs included in the plan this period (`-1` means unlimited).

active_jobs.title: Active Jobs
active_jobs.description: Number of permanent jobs (recurring and manual) counted against the plan's job limit.

active_jobs_limit.title: Active Jobs Limit
active_jobs_limit.description: Maximum permanent jobs the plan allows (`-1` means unlimited).

Usage.title: Usage
Usage.description: Current-period usage against the account's plan allotments.

UsageResource.title: UsageResource
UsageResource.description: JSON:API resource envelope for the usage report.
